message:
Welcome to Version Confusion, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once youre ready just click start to begin
Hey guys im seeing an issue with the website loading slowly
Anyone else experaning this?
No all ok for me
No issue for me
What browser and version of it are you using Tanya?
Im using chrome and let me check the version
Ok if it is V105.0 this is a recorded bug with a ticket in the backlog
Yeah it is V105.0
Should we not raise an incident for this? I know I can update the version and it will stop effecting me.
But could this not be effecting customers?
Had a look at the market share of people using this version it is only at 0.09%
Still not sure if it should not be an incidnt. @vallabh.vyas-dominos.co.uk What do you think?
let me look into this @tanya
@bob  does any user reported about this issue?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Let me gather reports
@tanya  is it just slowness. or you are not able to place orders also?
Already there is a bug ticket and i'm sure this might not be a bigger issue as well
There are no immediate calls as such - But I know there were a few calls here and there from the last 2 weeks

Here is the bug ticket details : 
Title  : Slow load times for users using V105.0 of chrome
Description : User have reported the frontend as loading slowly and with quick look at there set up appears to be an issue with frontend for users using V105.0 of chrome.
Reporter: User
Components : OB-Frontend
@bob  could you please confirm its just slowness and not drop in orders
For me orders are going well
Ohh the reports is actually showing that 20% of our customers are experiencing an issue where it is taking three times as long the website to load. Seeing a few timeouts occurring as well

Here's the list of recent changes
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZDQ1MTE3NWU5ZDYxNDA4MTkxZmM3ZGJmMGJhNmE1OTE
*Incident Number INC100099:* p3 Website loading slowly chrome  V105.0
:star: Incident record created
Really dont think we need an incident. As I said we have a bug already and really need to focus on delivery of new features.
I still dont think its worth investing our time into this
Is it a global issue ?
@bez @tinus  just for you information. website is loading slowly in chrome browser  V105.0. there is no impact on orders. we are investigating.
:star: Qualify the problem
there is already a bug ticket for the same. will update in next 5 minutes
:star: Track actions and time box them
If there is a bug ticket, then why hasn't it been actioned yet?
@daniel  but we need to see if there is any user impact. @bob  could you please confirm how many user has reported this issue. is it global or specific region
got some more data points . Can see that there has been a drop in sales of 10% compared to the average over a two week period
It is affecting customers globally. It is still limited to customers using Chrome
@vallabh.vyas-dominos.co.uk do you think any deployment might be causing this issue ?
@daniel  @bob  when this issue was first reported. before or after any deployment?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
I'm not on v105 of Chrome. So for me the orders are working fine
Started around 2 weeks back
looking into this @bez there has been a drop in sales of 10% compared to the average over a two week period
:star: Use metrics as evidence
But initially only 2-3 users reported this. Thats when Daniel logged this as a bug ticket
what was deployed that time @daniel  which could have impacted this?
next update in another 5 minutes.
Around 2 weeks back we didn't do anything related to this version
@daniel how much time it may take if we provide a hot fix?
Maybe platform might have done something
I think we can advice them to use other browser until we get to find some fix
@tanya  @hamed any changes done to platform ?
@daniel  how many day or hour it will take to provide fix?
:star: Manage actions and timings
Tanya is in best position to look into this issue as its a P3. I'll assist in case of further escalation
I can replicate the issue with v105
i used a different browser and had no issues
I'm checking this version of chrome right now
@tanya any changes to platform before this issue was reported 2 weeks back
Not sure on ETA for fix. I haven't had a look into this bug ticket
As it was a low priority
I don't think in that case any recent changes might be a concern
Have you found any working theories ?
so we have issue with specific chrome browser version and we have sales drop of 10% globally compared to last 2 weeks. and there were no code changes done.
i think i found something

@bez  Summary
so we have issue with specific chrome browser version and we have sales drop of 10% globally compared to last 2 weeks. and there were no code changes done.
the issue is not with Frontend but with an update made to the nginx web server version that does not support V105.0 of Chrome
This matches the time of the first report
@tanya  can you fix this ?
Do we have a workaround until you all work on fix ?
We have 2 options to resolve this - change the nginx web server version back or add a patch. Either of these will resolve the incident
@tanya  lets change the nginx web server version and add a patch
Doing it now
@bez it seems ngnix server changes were deployed which has impacted this. will give update in next 5 minutes
Do you have any ETA ?
I have changed the version and added the patch. Should be good now
@daniel  @tanya  could you test now on chrome and confirm
:star: Reproduces the incident
Sure let me check my v105 now
For me site works fine and no errors are there in logs
I can access the site now easily
fix is applied @bez  we are testing the changes
@bob  all good from customer side?
Ok great, confirm me again once you're confident
site is working fine now @bez
Checked with one of the user and he is able to see the site again
@bob shall we close the incident?
Seems this fix worked afterall
Yes you can close it now
let close the incident
Hey @vallabh.vyas-dominos.co.uk I have just retried the website with V105.0 of chrome and it seems the fix worked
Glad we created an incident for this. Need to review our training on when to create an incident or a bug but great work team.
Sorry guys should have raised an incident when first discovered.
We are all learning Daniel. We can carry the learnings foward
Just checked and it looks all good now team
Lets continue monitoring this and we can have a great conversation during the PIR

Thank you for playing Version Confusion, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit start when you are ready.
